connections.outputSignal: corpora[].packetPath with verified GC-047/GC-048/GC-050 verdicts and common facet schema from CI1 readiness packets
connections.routingRule: When two or more CI1 scan packets with COMPLETE_VERIFIED or CLOSED_PASS_BOUNDED status exist in the GC-051 registry, the CI1-T4 worker may normalize their common facet schemas into the cross-corpus index model JSON. The routing does not authorize autonomous mutation, runtime indexing, retrieval behavior, automatic roadmap creation, LPCI implementation, or public claims.
connections.claimBoundary: This connection proves typed artifact routing from CI1 scan packets to the cross-corpus index model. It does not prove semantic correctness, runtime retrieval behavior, automatic roadmap creation, autonomous mutation, LPCI implementation, or public claims.

connections.outputSignal: corpora[].findings[] with disposition, defectClass, learningLane, f2gRef/roadmapRef/workOrderRef action evidence
connections.routingRule: Every scan finding must carry F2G-compatible defectClass and learningLane; deferred or blocked scan findings must cite a real f2gRef, roadmapRef, or workOrderRef artifact.
connections.claimBoundary: This connection proves scan-finding traceability into F2G-compatible learning disposition; it does not prove semantic correctness, automatic roadmap creation, autonomous mutation, or runtime behavior change.

connections.outputSignal: commonFacets, normalizationRules, sourceMappings, downstreamRoutes from committed T4 cross-corpus index model with T4 CLOSED_PASS_BOUNDED status
connections.routingRule: When CI1-T4 is CLOSED_PASS_BOUNDED and the cross-corpus index model JSON is committed, the CI1-T5 worker may adversarially sample the model against classification ledgers and bounded source cross-checks inside the two registered pilot roots only. Output is a machine-readable sampling result for CI1-T6 checker decision. The routing does not authorize new legacy root scans, runtime indexing, retrieval implementation, LPCI implementation, provider calls, autonomous mutation, or public claims.
connections.claimBoundary: This connection proves typed artifact routing from the cross-corpus index model to the adversarial classification sampling result. It does not prove semantic correctness of classifications, runtime retrieval behavior, automatic roadmap creation, autonomous mutation, LPCI implementation, or public claims. Sampling verdict PASSED_SAMPLING_WITH_GAPS does not certify semantic correctness.

connections.outputSignal: normalizationGaps[], t6Inputs.structuralCheckCandidates[], t6Inputs.documentationOnlyGaps[] from CI1-T5 sampling with PASSED_SAMPLING_WITH_GAPS status
connections.routingRule: When CI1-T5 is CLOSED_PASS_BOUNDED and the sampling results JSON is committed, the CI1-T6 worker evaluates each normalization gap from T5 and assigns a governance decision (STRUCTURAL_CHECK_REQUIRED, STANDARD_REQUIRED_FIRST, VOCABULARY_EXTENSION_REQUIRED, DOCUMENTATION_ONLY, ACCEPT_NO_ACTION, or BLOCKED). Output is a structured per-gap decision table and a T7_READY or T7_HOLD gate verdict. The routing does not authorize checker code implementation, runtime source modification, LPCI implementation, provider calls, autonomous mutation, or public claims.
connections.claimBoundary: This connection proves typed artifact routing from the T5 classification sampling result to the T6 checker decision table. It does not prove checker implementation, runtime guard enforcement, semantic correctness of gap decisions, automatic roadmap creation, autonomous mutation, LPCI implementation, or public claims. T7_READY verdict unlocks CI1-T7 dispatch only after CI1-T6 artifacts are committed and the session front door is updated.

connections.outputSignal: NR-04/NR-05/NR-11 structural gate results from CI2-T2 checker trio (check_corpus_packet_source_hash.py, check_corpus_packet_normalized_path.py, check_corpus_packet_disposition_canonical.py)
connections.routingRule: When CI2-T2 NR-04/NR-05/NR-11 checkers are CLOSED_PASS_BOUNDED and wired into autorun, the CI2-T3 worker may create the enforced cross-corpus index model JSON that records field classification, checker bindings, and NR-11 alias vocabulary. The routing does not authorize LPCI runtime, vector database, embedding, live proof, public-sync, new corpus scan, or autonomous mutation.
connections.claimBoundary: This connection proves typed artifact routing from CI2-T2 packet normalization checkers to the CI2-T3 enforced index model. It does not prove LPCI implementation, runtime retrieval behavior, semantic correctness of field classifications, automatic roadmap creation, autonomous mutation, or public claims.

connections.outputSignal: per-gap decision table (6 rows), checker spec stubs, T7_READY gate verdict from CI1-T6 CLOSED_PASS_BOUNDED
connections.routingRule: When CI1-T6 is CLOSED_PASS_BOUNDED and the T7_READY gate is confirmed, the CI1-T7 worker may synthesize the CI1 chain (T2–T6) into a typed LPCI intake bridge specification. The bridge defines the corpus input contract, claim boundary inheritance, deferred gap obligations, governance gate requirements, and blocked scope for LPCI-T1. The routing does not authorize LPCI chatbot implementation, runtime route authoring, provider integration, embedding pipeline, vector database, UI components, live proof, autonomous mutation, or public claims. LPCI implementation requires a separate governed product roadmap after CI1-T7 closes.
connections.claimBoundary: This connection proves typed artifact routing from the CI1-T6 checker decision to the CI1-T7 LPCI intake bridge specification. It does not prove LPCI implementation, runtime retrieval behavior, chatbot capability, automatic roadmap creation, autonomous mutation, or public claims. CI1-T7 CLOSED_PASS_BOUNDED status unlocks LPCI product roadmap proposal only after the intake bridge is committed and the session front door is updated.

connections.routingRule: When ERH-T2A identifies routes with MISSING_ROUTE_GOVERNANCE_PROOF and ERH-T2C is authorized, the bounded worker may register those routes in ROUTE_GOVERNANCE_PROOF_REGISTRY and wire them through authorizeRouteGovernanceProof(). The checker gates that the workflow chain exists with a valid decision marker, the ledger has a machine marker, and the next-auth beta marker has not been silently removed without a separate migration work order. The routing does not authorize autonomous memory mutation, learning orchestration, runtime promotion, provider calls, or public claims.

The routing does not score semantic truth, authorize autonomous mutation, run providers, change runtime behavior, or make public claims.
